do: Derive values during render and lift shared state to the nearest common parent
dont: Don't mirror props into state and resync them
note: A single source of truth removes a whole class of stale-data bugs.

do: Reserve effects for syncing with external systems and return a cleanup
dont: Don't run user-event logic inside effects
note: If it reacts to a click, it belongs in the handler, not an effect.

do: Measure with the profiler before reaching for memoization
dont: Don't wrap everything in memo by reflex
note: Premature memoization adds noise without a measured win.

do: Keep components on the server and push interactivity to the leaves
dont: Don't mark a whole page client for one widget
note: A narrow client boundary keeps bundles small and data on the server.

do: Start independent fetches together in async server components
dont: Don't chain awaits that create request waterfalls
note: Parallel fetching cuts the time before content can stream.

do: Handle mutations with server actions and validate on the server
dont: Don't treat client validation as your only gate
note: Server validation is the boundary that actually protects data.

do: Treat generated components as your own code and edit them in place
dont: Don't wrap them in adapters to avoid touching the source
note: You own the files, so refactor directly instead of layering around them.

do: Drive colors through CSS variables so themes swap at the root
dont: Don't hardcode hex values inside components
note: Token-driven color lets dark mode and rebrands flow from one place.

do: Build on accessible primitives and keep their focus and aria wiring
dont: Don't strip ref forwarding or aria props when restyling
note: Those props are what keep the component usable by keyboard and screen reader.

do: Use computed for derived values and ref for primitives
dont: Don't recompute derived data inside a watcher
note: Computed caches and tracks dependencies so it recalculates only when needed.

do: Extract shared stateful logic into composables
dont: Don't duplicate the same ref and watcher logic across components
note: Composables package reactive behavior the way functions package plain logic.

do: Pass data down via props and signal changes up with emitted events
dont: Don't mutate a prop directly inside the child
note: One-way flow keeps the parent the clear owner of that state.

do: Render pages on the server and hydrate once on the client
dont: Don't gate first-paint content behind client-only fetching
note: Universal rendering gives fast first paint and good crawlability.

do: Fetch with the framework data utilities so payloads transfer to the client
dont: Don't call raw fetch and refetch the same data after hydration
note: Framework fetching dedupes the request across server and client.

do: Declare reactive state with runes and compute with derived
dont: Don't reach for stores when local runed state is enough
note: Fine-grained signals update only what changed without a virtual DOM diff.

do: Let effects auto-track their reads and return teardown
dont: Don't hand-list dependencies the runtime can infer
note: Auto-tracking keeps effects correct when the read set changes.

do: Destructure props and mark two-way ones bindable
dont: Don't fake child content with conditional markup
note: Snippets pass renderable chunks as first-class typed values.

do: Ship static HTML and hydrate only the interactive islands
dont: Don't hydrate a component that never changes after load
note: Selective hydration keeps the JavaScript payload near zero.

do: Model markdown content as typed collections with a schema
dont: Don't read content files by hand without validation
note: A schema turns malformed frontmatter into a build-time error.

do: Pick a hydration directive that matches when the widget is needed
dont: Don't default every island to hydrate on load
note: Deferring to visible or idle spreads work off the critical path.

do: Size components with container queries so they adapt to their slot
dont: Don't tie every component to global viewport breakpoints
note: Container queries let one component work in a sidebar or full page.

do: Extend the theme and consume the spacing and color scale
dont: Don't litter markup with arbitrary one-off pixel values
note: A shared scale keeps rhythm consistent across the interface.

do: Compose conditional classes with a typed variant helper
dont: Don't build class strings with sprawling ternary concatenation
note: A variant API makes the valid combinations explicit and lintable.

do: Define dark styles with the dark variant against semantic tokens
dont: Don't duplicate components just to swap colors
note: One markup path with variants halves the surface you maintain.

do: Own value state with State and pass write access via Binding
dont: Don't store the same value in two places and sync them
note: Clear ownership lets the framework invalidate exactly the right views.

do: Compose layout with stacks and let views size to their content
dont: Don't hardcode absolute frames to force a layout
note: Flexible sizing adapts across device sizes and Dynamic Type.

do: Give list rows stable identity and use lazy containers
dont: Don't build long scrolling content in an eager stack
note: Lazy containers realize rows only as they approach the viewport.

do: Keep widgets immutable and lift mutable state to a controller
dont: Don't mutate fields inside a stateless widget
note: Rebuilding from immutable inputs makes the UI a pure function of state.

do: Mark unchanging subtrees const so they skip rebuilds
dont: Don't rebuild the whole tree when one leaf changed
note: Const widgets are cached and excluded from the rebuild pass.

do: Design around the constraints-down sizes-up layout model
dont: Don't fight overflow with fixed pixel sizes everywhere
note: Understanding constraint flow prevents most overflow errors.

do: Lay out with flexbox and density-independent units
dont: Don't assume web pixel sizes map to device points
note: Density-independent units keep touch targets sized correctly across screens.

do: Render long lists with a virtualized list component
dont: Don't map a large array into a plain scroll view
note: Virtualization keeps memory flat by recycling off-screen rows.

do: Use a native-backed navigator for stack and gesture behavior
dont: Don't reimplement back gestures and transitions by hand
note: Native navigation matches platform muscle memory for free.

do: Model state with signals and derive with computed
dont: Don't call manual change detection to force updates
note: Signals let the framework update only the views that read them.

do: Build standalone components and run them on OnPush
dont: Don't rely on default change detection for large trees
note: OnPush plus signals narrows checks to components whose inputs changed.

do: Use typed reactive forms with composable validators
dont: Don't scatter validation logic across template directives
note: A reactive model keeps form state and rules testable in one place.
